annotations.label: Minkowski space

anchor.snippet: is the main mathematical description of spacetime in the absence of gravitation

annotations.note: No Minkowski/spacetime structure exists in Mathlib (grep for `Minkowski` finds only Minkowski's theorem, inequality and functional).

annotations.label: Spacetime interval / Minkowski norm squared

anchor.snippet: is a pseudo-Euclidean space equipped with an isotropic quadratic form called the spacetime interval

annotations.note: General quadratic forms and the (an)isotropy notion exist, but the specific indefinite Minkowski interval form is not defined.

annotations.label: Light cone

anchor.snippet: is on the null cone of the origin, called the light cone

annotations.note: No null cone / light cone of a Lorentzian form is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Minkowski inner product

anchor.snippet: the quadratic form is converted to a symmetric bilinear form called the Minkowski inner product

annotations.note: Polarization of a quadratic form to a symmetric bilinear form is general infrastructure, but the Minkowski form itself is not present.

annotations.label: Lorentz group

anchor.snippet: that preserves the spacetime interval

annotations.note: Isometries of a bilinear form exist generally, but the Lorentz group O(3,1) of an indefinite form is not defined (only the Euclidean `Matrix.orthogonalGroup`).

annotations.label: Timelike, spacelike, null vectors

anchor.snippet: A vector is timelike if

annotations.note: Causal classification of vectors by sign of a Lorentzian form is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Light cone of an event

anchor.snippet: The set of all null vectors at an event

annotations.note: No notion of the null-vector set at an event is formalized.

annotations.label: Future- and past-directed timelike vectors

anchor.snippet: future-directed timelike vectors whose first component is positive

annotations.note: Time orientation of timelike vectors is not formalized.

annotations.label: Three classes of null vectors

anchor.snippet: Null vectors fall into three classes

annotations.note: Classification of null vectors is not formalized.

annotations.label: Composition of an orthonormal basis

anchor.snippet: necessarily consists of one timelike and three spacelike unit vectors

annotations.note: This is Sylvester's law of inertia (signature is basis-independent), whose uniqueness part is formalized, though not specialized to the (1,3) Minkowski case.

annotations.label: Null basis

anchor.snippet: basis consisting entirely of null vectors, called a null basis

annotations.note: No notion of a null (light-cone) basis is formalized.

annotations.label: Timelike/spacelike/null vector fields

anchor.snippet: Vector fields are called timelike, spacelike, or null

annotations.note: Causal classification of vector fields is not formalized (no Lorentzian metric).

annotations.label: Convexity of the light cones

anchor.snippet: both forward and backward cones are convex, whereas the space-like region is not convex

annotations.note: Convexity of the Minkowski timelike cones is not proven (general `ConvexCone` exists but not this statement).

annotations.label: Scalar product of time-like vectors

anchor.snippet: The scalar product of two time-like vectors

annotations.note: The Minkowski scalar product restricted to timelike vectors is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: The scalar product of two time-like vectors

annotations.label: Positivity of the scalar product

anchor.snippet: the scalar product of two similarly directed time-like vectors is always positive

annotations.note: This Lorentzian-specific positivity result is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Zero scalar product implies space-like

anchor.snippet: if the scalar product of two vectors is zero, then one of these, at least, must be space-like

annotations.note: This Lorentzian-specific result is not formalized.

annotations.label: Positive sum of similarly directed time-like vectors

anchor.snippet: a linear sum with positive coefficients of similarly directed time-like vectors is also similarly directed

annotations.note: Closure of the future timelike cone under positive combinations is not formalized.

annotations.label: Norm of a time-like vector

anchor.snippet: The norm of a time-like vector

annotations.note: The Lorentzian norm of timelike vectors is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: The norm of a time-like vector

annotations.label: Reversed Cauchy inequality

anchor.snippet: The reversed Cauchy inequality is another consequence of the convexity

annotations.note: Only the ordinary Cauchy–Schwarz inequality for inner product spaces is in Mathlib, not the reversed Lorentzian version.

anchors.snippet: The reversed Cauchy inequality is another consequence of the convexity

annotations.label: Reversed triangle inequality

anchor.snippet: For two similarly directed time-like vectors u and w , the inequality is

annotations.note: The reversed triangle inequality for timelike vectors is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: For two similarly directed time-like vectors u and w , the inequality is

annotations.label: Minkowski space as a vector space with bilinear form

anchor.snippet: is a 4 -dimensional real vector space equipped with a non-degenerate, symmetric bilinear form

annotations.note: Non-degenerate symmetric bilinear forms on a vector space are fully developed, but no 4-dimensional Minkowski instance is defined.

annotations.label: Canonical identification of tangent vectors

anchor.snippet: allows for the canonical identification of vectors in tangent spaces

annotations.note: For a normed/vector space modeled manifold the `TangentSpace` is canonically the space itself, but this is generic, not Minkowski-specific.

anchors.snippet: allows for the canonical identification of vectors in tangent spaces

annotations.label: Tangent vector as a column vector under Lorentz transformation

anchor.snippet: A tangent vector at a point p may be defined

annotations.note: The transformation-law definition of a tangent vector under Lorentz transformations is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: A tangent vector at a point p may be defined

annotations.label: Metric signature

anchor.snippet: The metric signature refers to which sign the Minkowski inner product yields

annotations.note: The signature (`sigPos`/`sigNeg`) of a quadratic form over a linearly ordered field is formalized as the general concept.

annotations.label: Minkowski metric tensor

anchor.snippet: is a tensor of type (0,2) at each point in spacetime, called the Minkowski metric

annotations.note: The Minkowski metric tensor field is not formalized.

annotations.label: Lorentzian manifold

anchor.snippet: a Lorentzian manifold L is likewise equipped with a metric tensor g

annotations.note: Mathlib only has positive-definite Riemannian metrics; Lorentzian (indefinite-signature) metrics are not formalized.

annotations.label: Minkowski space as pseudo-Euclidean space

anchor.snippet: is thus a pseudo-Euclidean space with total dimension n = 4 and signature

annotations.note: Non-degenerate symmetric bilinear forms with a notion of signature exist, but no pseudo-Euclidean Minkowski space is constructed.

annotations.label: Events

anchor.snippet: Elements of Minkowski space are called events

annotations.note: No Minkowski space, hence no notion of events.

annotations.label: Metric on abstract vector space V

anchor.snippet: the metric is a bilinear form on an abstract four-dimensional real vector space V

annotations.note: Bilinear forms on an abstract vector space are the `LinearMap.BilinForm` type, but the specific 4-dimensional Minkowski metric is not defined.

anchors.snippet: the metric is a bilinear form on an abstract four-dimensional real vector space V

annotations.label: Born coordinates and light-cone coordinates

anchor.snippet: An interesting example of non-inertial coordinates for (part of) Minkowski spacetime is the Born coordinates

annotations.note: Born/light-cone coordinate systems are not formalized.

annotations.label: Minkowski inner product is indefinite

anchor.snippet: The Minkowski inner product is not an inner product , since it has non-zero null vectors

annotations.note: Positive-definiteness/anisotropy of forms is formalized (indefinite = not `PosDef`), but the Minkowski case is not stated.

annotations.label: Minkowski metric as metric tensor

anchor.snippet: The Minkowski metric η is the metric tensor of Minkowski space

annotations.note: The Minkowski metric tensor η is not formalized.

annotations.label: Minkowski inner product definition

anchor.snippet: yields an inner product-like structure on M , previously and also henceforth, called the Minkowski inner product

annotations.note: The general symmetric bilinear form structure exists, but the Minkowski inner product itself is not defined.

anchors.snippet: yields an inner product-like structure on M , previously and also henceforth, called the Minkowski inner product

annotations.label: Minkowski norm squared

anchor.snippet: the resulting quantity will be called the Minkowski norm squared

annotations.note: The quadratic form v ↦ B(v,v) of a bilinear form is general infrastructure, but the Minkowski norm squared is not defined.

anchors.snippet: the resulting quantity will be called the Minkowski norm squared

annotations.label: Invariance of inner product under Lorentz transformations

anchor.snippet: these are quantities unaffected by Lorentz transformations

annotations.note: Isometries of a bilinear form preserve it by definition; the Lorentz-specific statement is not present.

annotations.label: Defining property of a Lorentz transformation

anchor.snippet: the defining property of a Lorentz transformation in that it preserves the inner product

annotations.note: A form-preserving linear map is `BilinForm.Isometry`; the Lorentz group itself is not defined.

annotations.label: Orthogonality of events

anchor.snippet: Two events u and v are orthogonal when the bilinear form is zero for them

annotations.note: `IsOrtho B x y ↔ B x y = 0` is exactly orthogonality with respect to a bilinear form, generalizing the Minkowski case.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic orthogonality

anchor.snippet: then the relation is hyperbolic orthogonality

annotations.note: Orthogonality wrt an indefinite form is captured by `IsOrtho`, but the rapidity/hyperbolic-angle interpretation is not formalized.

annotations.label: Rapidity

anchor.snippet: has been labelled rapidity since it is associated with the speed of the frame

annotations.note: Rapidity (Lorentz-boost hyperbolic angle) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Spacetime interval

anchor.snippet: the spacetime interval between two arbitrary events called 1 and 2 is

annotations.note: The spacetime interval between two events is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: the spacetime interval between two arbitrary events called 1 and 2 is

annotations.label: Invariance of the interval under linear transformations

anchor.snippet: The invariance of the interval under coordinate transformations between inertial frames

annotations.note: Invariance of a form under its isometries is general infrastructure; the interval invariance statement is not present.

anchors.snippet: The invariance of the interval under coordinate transformations between inertial frames

annotations.label: Standard (orthonormal) basis

anchor.snippet: A standard or orthonormal basis for Minkowski space is a set of four mutually orthogonal vectors

annotations.note: Orthogonal families/bases with respect to a bilinear form are formalized, but the Minkowski standard basis with fixed normalization is not.

anchors.snippet: A standard or orthonormal basis for Minkowski space is a set of four mutually orthogonal vectors

annotations.label: Timelike and spatial components

anchor.snippet: The component v 0 is called the timelike component of v while the other three components are called the spatial components

annotations.note: The timelike/spatial component terminology is not formalized.

annotations.label: Standard bases related by Lorentz transformation

anchor.snippet: Any two such bases are related in some sense by a Lorentz transformation

annotations.note: No Lorentz group, so this transitivity statement is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: Any two such bases are related in some sense by a Lorentz transformation

annotations.label: Non-degenerate form gives map to dual

anchor.snippet: a non-degenerate bilinear form provides a map between a vector space and its dual

annotations.note: `BilinForm.toDual` gives the linear equivalence V ≃ₗ Module.Dual K V from a non-degenerate form on a finite-dimensional space.

annotations.label: Covariant vectors

anchor.snippet: vectors with lower indices are referred to as covariant vectors

annotations.note: Covectors are `Module.Dual`, though the index/covariant terminology is not used.

annotations.label: Contravariant vectors

anchor.snippet: The ones with upper indices are contravariant vectors

annotations.note: Contravariant vectors are simply module elements; no specific declaration or index terminology exists.

annotations.label: Raising of an index

anchor.snippet: can be used to define raising of an index

annotations.note: Raising an index is the inverse `(B.toDual hB).symm : Dual → V`, but the tensor-index formalism is not present.

annotations.label: Lowered vector via partial evaluation

anchor.snippet: the lowered version of a vector can be thought of as the partial evaluation

annotations.note: The lowered vector is the partial evaluation `B x : Module.Dual`, packaged as `B.toDual`.

anchors.snippet: the lowered version of a vector can be thought of as the partial evaluation

annotations.label: Non-degeneracy equals injectivity of partial evaluation

anchor.snippet: Non-degeneracy is then equivalent to injectivity of the partial evaluation map

annotations.note: Non-degeneracy is proven equivalent to the kernel of the partial-evaluation map being trivial (i.e. injectivity).

anchors.snippet: Non-degeneracy is then equivalent to injectivity of the partial evaluation map

annotations.label: Exterior derivative as covector field

anchor.snippet: The exterior derivative d f of a function f is a covector field

annotations.note: The differential `mfderiv` assigns a cotangent covector to each point, but Mathlib has no general differential-forms / exterior-derivative framework.

anchors.snippet: The exterior derivative d f of a function f is a covector field

annotations.label: Vector field

anchor.snippet: A vector field is an assignment of a tangent vector to each point p

annotations.note: Vector fields are sections `Π x, TangentSpace I x` of the tangent bundle (with `ContMDiffSection` for smoothness).

anchors.snippet: A vector field is an assignment of a tangent vector to each point p

annotations.label: Chronological precedence

anchor.snippet: x chronologically precedes y if y − x is future-directed timelike

annotations.note: The chronological order relation is not formalized.

annotations.label: Causal precedence

anchor.snippet: x causally precedes y if y − x is future-directed null or future-directed timelike

annotations.note: The causal order relation is not formalized.

annotations.label: Simultaneous hyperplane

anchor.snippet: the simultaneous hyperplane for x is

annotations.note: The simultaneity hyperplane is not formalized.

annotations.label: Lorentzian manifold as generalization

anchor.snippet: A Lorentzian manifold is a generalization of Minkowski space in two ways

annotations.note: Lorentzian manifolds are not formalized; Mathlib only has positive-definite Riemannian metrics.

annotations.label: Complexified Minkowski space

anchor.snippet: Complexified Minkowski space is defined as

annotations.note: Complexified Minkowski space is not formalized.

annotations.label: Inner product on complexified Minkowski space

anchor.snippet: The inner product -like structure on M c is defined as

annotations.note: The bilinear extension to complexified Minkowski space is not formalized.

annotations.label: Relativistic pure spin

anchor.snippet: A relativistic pure spin of an electron or any half spin particle is described by

annotations.note: This physics example is not formalized.

annotations.label: n-dimensional Minkowski space

anchor.snippet: n -dimensional Minkowski space is a vector space of real dimension n

annotations.note: Vector spaces with a non-degenerate symmetric bilinear form (any dimension) are formalized, but no Minkowski instance with signature (n−1,1) is defined.

annotations.label: String theory and M-theory

anchor.snippet: String theory and M-theory are two examples where n > 4

annotations.note: These physics examples are not formalized.

annotations.label: de Sitter space as submanifold

anchor.snippet: de Sitter space can be formulated as a submanifold of generalized Minkowski space

annotations.note: de Sitter space is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Flat spacetime obeys the Pythagorean theorem

anchor.snippet: the three spatial components of Minkowski spacetime always obey the Pythagorean theorem

annotations.note: This is a physical statement about spatial flatness of spacetime; not formalized (general Euclidean Pythagoras exists but is unrelated to this claim).

annotations.label: Local approximation by Minkowski space

anchor.snippet: Minkowski space is still a good description in an infinitesimal region surrounding any point

annotations.note: Local flatness of a Lorentzian manifold is not formalized.

annotations.label: Minkowski space is not a metric or Riemannian space

anchor.snippet: not a metric space and not a Riemannian manifold with a Riemannian metric

annotations.note: This negative statement about Minkowski space is not formalized.

annotations.label: Isometric embedding of hyperbolic spaces

anchor.snippet: Hyperbolic spaces can be isometrically embedded in spaces of one more dimension

annotations.note: No hyperbolic space embedding into Minkowski space is formalized.

annotations.label: Hyperboloid model of hyperbolic space

anchor.snippet: the hyperboloid model of hyperbolic space

annotations.note: The hyperboloid model is not formalized (grep for `hyperboloid` finds nothing).

anchors.snippet: the hyperboloid model of hyperbolic space

annotations.label: Constant negative curvature of the hyperboloid

anchor.snippet: It is a space of constant negative curvature

annotations.note: Constant negative curvature of the hyperboloid model is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: It is a space of constant negative curvature

annotations.label: Behavior of tensors under inclusion

anchor.snippet: For inclusion maps from a submanifold S into M and a covariant tensor

annotations.note: Pullback of covariant tensors under inclusions is not formalized (no general tensor-field pullback).

anchors.snippet: For inclusion maps from a submanifold S into M and a covariant tensor

annotations.label: Pullback of a covariant tensor

anchor.snippet: The pullback of a covariant k -tensor

annotations.note: Mathlib has pullback of vector fields (`VectorField.mpullback`) but no pullback of covariant k-tensors / differential forms.

anchors.snippet: The pullback of a covariant k -tensor

annotations.label: Pushforward of vectors

anchor.snippet: of a vector field under a map F : M → N between manifolds is defined by

annotations.note: The pointwise pushforward T_pM → T_{F(p)}N is `mfderiv I I' F p`, though pushforward of an entire vector field is not packaged.

anchors.snippet: of a vector field under a map F : M → N between manifolds is defined by

annotations.label: Parametrization of a submanifold

anchor.snippet: A parametrization of a submanifold S of a manifold M is a map

annotations.note: No named notion of a submanifold parametrization exists (only immersions/embeddings/charts as related infrastructure).

annotations.label: Hyperbolic stereographic projection

anchor.snippet: Stereographic projection σ

annotations.note: Only the Euclidean stereographic projection from a sphere is formalized, not the hyperbolic (hyperboloid-to-ball) version.

anchors.snippet: Stereographic projection σ

annotations.label: Pulled-back metric is the Poincaré ball metric

anchor.snippet: the metric on the ball is identical to the Riemannian metric

annotations.note: The Poincaré ball model and this pulled-back-metric identity are not formalized.
